2605.12562.title: Uncovering Latent Pathological Signatures in Pulmonary CT via Cross-Window Knowledge Distillation
2605.12562.paper_summary: Multi-window CT imaging captures complementary pathological information across anatomical structures of differing densities, yet existing deep learning methods fuse representations only at later stages, missing cross-density interactions. We propose a cross-window knowledge distillation framework in which student encoders learn latent clinical priors from a teacher trained on the most informative window. Evaluated retrospectively on three cohorts - COPD-CT-DF (n=719), RSNA PE (n=1,433), and an in-house CTEPD dataset (n=161) - distillation improved per-window AUC by 10.1-16.5 percentage points on COPD-CT-DF (0.75-0.81 to 0.90-0.94; all P<0.001), with ensemble AUC reaching 0.9960. Similar gains were observed on RSNA PE (0.80-0.83 to 0.90-0.92) and CTEPD (AUC 0.7481 vs. 0.6264). Cross-window distillation internalises pathological signatures invisible to supervised approaches, offering a generalisable solution for multi-window pulmonary CT analysis.
2605.12562.paper_summary_zh: 多窗口 CT 成像捕捉不同密度解剖結構的互補病理信息，然而現有的深度學習方法僅在後期融合表示，錯過了跨密度的互動。我們提出了一個跨窗口知識蒸餾框架，在該框架中，學生編碼器從在最具信息窗口上訓練的教師中學習潛在的臨床先驗。對三個隊列進行回顧性評估 - COPD-CT-DF (n=719)、RSNA PE (n=1,433) 和一個內部 CTEPD 數據集 (n=161) - 蒸餾在 COPD-CT-DF 上改善了每窗口 AUC 10.1-16.5 個百分點 (0.75-0.81 提升至 0.90-0.94；所有 P<0.001)，集成 AUC 達到 0.9960。在 RSNA PE (0.80-0.83 提升至 0.90-0.92) 和 CTEPD (AUC 0.7481 對 0.6264) 上也觀察到了類似的增益。跨窗口蒸餾內化了監督方法無法察覺的病理特徵，為多窗口肺部 CT 分析提供了一個可泛化的解決方案。
2605.12562.author: Bo Peng et.al.
2605.12562.authors: Bo Peng, Wujian Xu, Kun Wang, Ximing Liao, Na Wang, Daqian Shi, Tian Li, Jing Gao, Johan Thygesen, Yingqun Ji, Honghan Wu
